title: CSS Animation & Motion Design Specialist Assessment
description: Expert-level certification for UI/UX developers specializing in high-performance CSS animations, SVG manipulation, easing functions, and browser rendering optimization.

questions.text: Which CSS properties are considered 'cheap' to animate because they do not trigger layout or paint cycles?
questions.explanation: Transform and opacity can be handled by the GPU (compositor thread), avoiding the expensive 'Layout' and 'Paint' steps.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `will-change` property?
options: To hint to the browser to optimize for upcoming property changes
questions.explanation: It prepares the browser for animations by creating a new layer, but should be used sparingly.

questions.text: In `cubic-bezier(x1, y1, x2, y2)`, what do the x-values represent?
questions.explanation: X-coordinates represent time and must be between 0 and 1.

questions.text: Which property retains styles of the last keyframe after an animation ends?
questions.explanation: Forwards ensures the element stays at the 100% keyframe state.

questions.text: What does the `steps()` timing function achieve?
questions.explanation: Ideal for sprite-sheets or 'ticking' effects.

questions.text: What is 'Layout Thrashing'?
options: Rapidly reading/writing DOM layout properties in JS
questions.explanation: Forcing the browser to recalculate layout multiple times per frame kills performance.

questions.text: How do you animate a 'drawing' SVG path effect?
questions.explanation: This involves offsetting a dash that matches the path length.

questions.text: Which media query respects users with vestibular disorders?
questions.explanation: Allows developers to provide simplified motion for sensitive users.

questions.text: What is the default value for `animation-fill-mode`?
questions.explanation: By default, animations do not apply any styles before or after the execution.

questions.text: To create a 3D effect, which property is required on the parent container?
questions.explanation: Perspective determines the distance between the z=0 plane and the user.

questions.text: What does `backface-visibility: hidden` prevent?
options: Seeing the element when rotated 180 degrees
questions.explanation: Useful for card-flip animations where the 'back' should be invisible.

questions.text: Which timing function is equivalent to `cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1.0)`?
questions.explanation: 'ease' is the default CSS timing function.

questions.text: What happens when you animate `box-shadow`?
questions.explanation: Box-shadow is expensive because it requires a repaint cycle on every frame.

questions.text: Which property allows an animation to run in reverse on every even cycle?
questions.explanation: Alternate cycles between 0-100% and 100-0%.

questions.text: What is the benefit of animating `transform: translate3d(0,0,0)` even if not moving in 3D?
questions.explanation: It often triggers the creation of a compositor layer (though will-change is more modern).

questions.text: If two animations change the same property, which one wins?
questions.explanation: Standard CSS cascade rules apply; the last definition overrides previous ones.

questions.text: What does `animation-play-state` control?
questions.explanation: Allows toggling between 'paused' and 'running', often used with JS.

questions.text: To animate a property over 2 seconds with a 1 second delay, what is the shorthand order?
questions.explanation: The first value that can be parsed as a time is duration; the second is delay.

questions.text: What is 'Staggered Animation'?
options: Multiple elements animating with incremental delays
questions.explanation: Creating a 'wave' effect by adding slight delay increments to a list of items.

questions.text: Which CSS property changes the origin point of a transformation?
questions.explanation: Default is 50% 50% (center).

questions.text: What does `transform-style: preserve-3d` do?
options: Allows child elements to exist in 3D space relative to the parent
questions.explanation: Without this, children are flattened into the 2D plane of the parent.

questions.text: How do you apply a CSS animation only when a user hovers?
questions.explanation: Adding the animation property to :hover triggers it when the cursor enters.

questions.text: What is the 'Composite' stage in rendering?
options: Combining layers into the final screen image
questions.explanation: The final stage where the browser puts pre-painted layers together.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a valid `animation-timing-function` keyword?
questions.explanation: Fast-forward is not a standard CSS easing keyword.

questions.text: What is a 'Keyframe'?
options: A specific point in an animation sequence defining styles
questions.explanation: @keyframes define the states at specific percentages of the duration.
